data.content:
### Create Interactive Bar Chart with Recharts and shadcn/ui

Source: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart

A Next.js client component that renders an interactive bar chart displaying desktop and mobile usage data. Uses Recharts for charting and shadcn/ui Card and Chart components for styling and container management. Includes ChartTooltip for hover interactions and CartesianGrid for visual reference lines.

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import { Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid, XAxis } from "recharts"

import {
  Card,
  CardContent,
  CardDescription,
  CardHeader,
  CardTitle,
} from "@/components/ui/card"
import {
  ChartContainer,
  ChartTooltip,
  ChartTooltipContent,
  type ChartConfig,
} from "@/components/ui/chart"

export const description = "An interactive bar chart"

const chartData = [
  { date: "2024-04-01", desktop: 222, mobile: 150 },
  { date: "2024-04-02", desktop: 97, mobile: 180 },
  { date: "2024-04-03", desktop: 167, mobile: 120 },
```

--------------------------------

### Render a Bar Chart with shadcn/ui Chart Components (TypeScript/React)

Source: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart

This example demonstrates how to create a bar chart using the `shadcn/ui` `ChartContainer` and `ChartTooltip` components in conjunction with `Recharts` `BarChart` and `Bar`. It shows the basic structure for composing a chart, where `data` is expected to be provided. The chart uses custom tooltip content provided by `ChartTooltipContent`.

```tsx
import { Bar, BarChart } from "recharts"

import { ChartContainer, ChartTooltipContent } from "@/components/ui/chart"

export function MyChart() {
  return (
    <ChartContainer>
      <BarChart data={data}>
        <Bar dataKey="value" />
        <ChartTooltip content={<ChartTooltipContent />} />
      </BarChart>
    </ChartContainer>
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define Chart Data in TypeScript

Source: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart

This snippet defines a constant array `chartData` representing monthly user statistics for desktop and mobile. It serves as the foundational data source for a Recharts bar chart, showing how to structure data for charting components.

```tsx
const chartData = [
  { month: "January", desktop: 186, mobile: 80 },
  { month: "February", desktop: 305, mobile: 200 },
  { month: "March", desktop: 237, mobile: 120 },
  { month: "April", desktop: 73, mobile: 190 },
  { month: "May", desktop: 209, mobile: 130 },
  { month: "June", desktop: 214, mobile: 140 },
]
```

--------------------------------

### Import CartesianGrid for Recharts Bar Chart in TypeScript

Source: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart

This snippet demonstrates how to import the `CartesianGrid` component from the 'recharts' library. This component is essential for adding a grid overlay to a chart, improving readability and data comparison.

```tsx
import { Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid } from "recharts"
```

--------------------------------

### Integrate CartesianGrid into Recharts Bar Chart in TypeScript

Source: https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/chart

This snippet shows how to add the `CartesianGrid` component within the `BarChart` component's JSX structure. By setting `vertical={false}`, it configures the grid to display only horizontal lines, enhancing the visual presentation of the bar chart.

```tsx
<ChartContainer config={chartConfig} className="min-h-[200px] w-full">
  <BarChart accessibilityLayer data={chartData}>
    <CartesianGrid vertical={false} />
    <Bar dataKey="desktop" fill="var(--color-desktop)" radius={4} />
    <Bar dataKey="mobile" fill="var(--color-mobile)" radius={4} />
  </BarChart>
</ChartContainer>
```
